mission.title: operator-framework: Metrics of the Cluster scoped operator causes can't list unstructured object errors
mission.description: Metrics of the Cluster scoped operator causes can't list unstructured object errors. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify operator-framework troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your operator-framework deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n operator-framework -l app.kubernetes.io/name=operator-framework
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=operator-framework -n operator-framework --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review operator-framework configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant operator-framework configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n operator-framework -l app.kubernetes.io/name=operator-framework
kubectl get configmap -n operator-framework -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=operator-framework
```
**What did you do?**

Created an operator with operator sdk.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Metrics of the Cluster scoped operator causes can't list…
steps.description:
I could check and reproduce which shows be this scenario.  Following the detailed information. 

**Scenario**
- Create a go project as described in the [QuickStart](https://github.com/operator-framework/operator-sdk#create-and-deploy-an-app-operator)
- Then, apply the required changes for the
```yaml
E0822 17:26:58.302751       1 reflector.go:134] pkg/mod/k8s.io/client-go@v0.0.0-20190228174230-b40b2a5939e4/tools/cache/reflector.go:95: Failed to list *unstructured.Unstructured: the server could not find the requested resource
```

steps.title: Confirm Metrics of the Cluster scoped operator causes… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=operator-framework -n operator-framework --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n operator-framework --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
I could check and reproduce which shows be this scenario.  Following the detailed information. 

**Scenario**
- Create a go project as described in the [QuickStart](https://github.com/operator-framework/operator-sdk#create-and-deploy-an-app-operator)
- Then, apply the required changes for the operator to become cluster-scope.
codeSnippets: E0822 17:26:58.302751       1 reflector.go:134] pkg/mod/k8s.io/client-go@v0.0.0-20190228174230-b40b2a5939e4/tools/cache/reflector.go:95: Failed to list *unstructured.Unstructured: the server could not find the requested resource

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with operator-framework installed or the issue environment reproducible.
